status: targeted synthetic correlated-noise validation; not TESS flight-data evidence
validated_components: recovery versus empirically significant recovery separation
interpretation: The moving-block bootstrap preserved the fixture scatter distribution without forcing dimming/brightening symmetry.
A nominal white-noise five-sigma threshold was exceeded in every correlated-noise surrogate, demonstrating that raw detector SNR is not a calibrated false-alarm probability.
HOU-EARTH now compares recovered signals with per-target full-search surrogate maxima using an add-one finite-sample p-value.
With 64 null trials the minimum resolvable empirical p-value is 1/65 = 0.015384615384615385; the pilot supports a 0.05 screen but not a 0.01 claim.

pilot_null_policy.reason: Known transit hosts remain valid physical-injection backgrounds but are not represented as no-event surrogate controls. Unmasked null targets retain real stellar and instrumental excursions rather than optimistically removing every detected event.

limitations: The benchmark is synthetic correlated noise, not TESS flight data.
The injected correlation process is one AR-like fixture and does not span TESS systematics.
The three pilot null targets are not certified signal-free; their surrogates estimate an empirical background under the stated policy.
Full repository CI and the six-target TESS workflow were still queued without a hosted runner at the time this manifest was frozen.
